title: TaskHub Actions
description: Defines actions, tasks, and pipelines for the TaskHub extension.

id.description: Unique identifier for the action (e.g., button.build, pipeline.analyze).

title.description: The label displayed in the UI for this action.

type.description: Defines special item types like separators or folders.

children.description: If type is 'folder', this contains nested action items.

description.description: A short explanation shown in the UI for what this action does.

tasks.description: A list of one or more tasks to execute in sequence.

successMessage.description: A message to show when the entire task sequence (pipeline) completes successfully.

failMessage.description: A message to show if any task in the sequence fails.

id.description: Unique identifier for the task within the pipeline. Used to reference its output.

type.description: The type of the task to execute.

command.description: The command to execute. Can be a direct string or an OS-specific object. Required for 'shell' and 'command' types.

env.description: Environment variables to set for the task. Values support interpolation.

cwd.description: Current working directory for the command. Defaults to ${workspaceFolder}.

revealTerminal.description: Controls terminal visibility for 'shell' type tasks.

options.description: Options for 'fileDialog' or 'folderDialog' types, based on VS Code's OpenDialogOptions.

placeHolder.description: The placeholder text for 'inputBox' or 'quickPick' type.

password.description: If true, the input box will be masked (for 'inputBox' type).

prefix.description: Text to prepend to the user input (for 'inputBox' type). The final value will be: prefix + userInput + suffix

suffix.description: Text to append to the user input (for 'inputBox' type). The final value will be: prefix + userInput + suffix

oneOf.description: Array of simple string items for 'quickPick' type.
Array of quick pick items with labels and optional descriptions.

items.description: List of items to select from (for 'quickPick' type).

canPickMany.description: If true, allows multiple selection (for 'quickPick' type). Results will be available as both 'value' (first selection) and 'values' (comma-separated list).

message.description: The confirmation message to display (for 'confirm' type). Supports variable interpolation.

confirmLabel.description: Label for the confirm button (for 'confirm' type). Defaults to 'Yes'.

cancelLabel.description: Label for the cancel button (for 'confirm' type). Defaults to 'No'.

oneOf.description: A direct path to the tool executable.
OS-specific paths for the tool executable.

tool.description: Path to the tool executable. Can be a direct string or an OS-specific object. Optional for 'zip' and 'unzip' types: if omitted, the built-in zip engine is used (.zip archives only). Specify a tool (e.g. 7z path) for other formats.

inputs.description: Specifies inputs from previous tasks' outputs. e.g., {'archive': 'zipTaskId', 'destination': 'folderDialogTaskId'}

file.description: Legacy alias for 'archive'. References a previous task that provides an archive path.

archive.description: References a previous task that provides the archive path (e.g., fileDialog or zip task).

destination.description: References a previous task that provides a destination folder path.

archive.description: For 'unzip' tasks, specifies the archive to extract. For 'zip' tasks, specifies the output archive path. Supports interpolation.

destination.description: For 'unzip' tasks, a string (supports interpolation) specifying the destination directory.

source.description: File or directory paths to be compressed. Required for 'zip' type.

function.description: The function to apply for 'stringManipulation' type (stripExtension, basename, basenameWithoutExtension, dirname, extension, toLowerCase, toUpperCase, trim).

passTheResultToNextTask.description: Defaults to false. If true, the task output (stdout) is captured to be used as a variable in subsequent tasks (e.g., ${task.id.output}). If false or omitted, output is streamed live to the terminal and is not captured.

isOneShot.description: If true, the task is considered successful as soon as it starts. Useful for non-terminating processes like GUI applications. Only works when `passTheResultToNextTask` is false.

path.description: Destination path for 'writeFile' / 'appendFile' tasks. Supports variable interpolation. Relative paths resolve against the action's workspace folder and must stay inside it.

content.description: Content to write for 'writeFile' / 'appendFile' tasks. Supports variable interpolation.

encoding.description: File encoding for 'writeFile' / 'appendFile' tasks. Defaults to 'utf8'. 'utf8bom' prefixes a BOM (on appendFile, only when the target file does not already exist). 'ascii' replaces non-ASCII characters with '?'.

eol.description: Line-ending normalization for 'writeFile' / 'appendFile' tasks. Defaults to 'keep'.

overwrite.description: For 'writeFile': if false, the task fails when the target file already exists. Defaults to true. Ignored for 'appendFile'.

mkdirs.description: For 'writeFile' / 'appendFile': if true (default), missing parent directories are created automatically. If false, the task fails when the parent directory is missing.

timeoutSeconds.description: Task-level timeout in seconds. If the task does not complete within this budget, it is canceled and the pipeline fails with a timeout error (subject to 'continueOnError'). 0 or omitted disables the timeout. Applies to every task type, including interactive ones.

continueOnError.description: If true, the pipeline continues to the next task when this task fails (including timeouts and user-canceled dialogs). The failing task's result becomes empty ({}). Defaults to false.

mode.description: Defines how to handle the task's output. Optional when only 'capture' is used.

language.description: Language mode for the editor tab when mode is 'editor'.

filePath.description: The path to save the output to when mode is 'file'. Can use variables.

content.description: The content to write to the file. Defaults to the task's stdout.

overwrite.description: If true, overwrite the target when mode is 'file'. Defaults to false. Can be a boolean or a string variable (e.g., '${someVar}') that evaluates to 'true'.

capture.description: Extract named variables from the task's string output. Each rule adds a `${task_id.<name>}` variable for downstream tasks. Only applies when the task returns a string output (shell/command with `passTheResultToNextTask: true`, or `stringManipulation`).

name.description: Variable name to expose as ${task_id.<name>}. Must be a valid identifier and must not shadow built-in keys (output, path, value, etc.).

regex.description: Regex pattern matched against the task output. If it matches, the specified capture group (default 1) is used.

group.description: Capture group index for `regex`. Defaults to 1. Use 0 to get the full match.

flags.description: Optional regex flags (e.g., 'm' for multiline, 'i' for case-insensitive).

line.description: Select one line by 0-based index. Negative values count from the end (-1 = last line). Ignored if `regex` is also provided.

trim.description: If true, apply trim() to the selected value.
